model.label: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview
model.description: Google • Preview Gemini model tuned for high-end multimodal reasoning and long-context analysis.

pick.rationale: In this highly anticipated Round of 32 matchup, Alabama and Texas Tech enter with nearly identical statistical profiles, both ranking inside the top 20 in KenPom and Torvik. The game projects as a fascinating clash, pitting the Crimson Tide's elite third-ranked offense against the Red Raiders' balanced top-30 units on both ends of the floor. While Texas Tech features a highly efficient scoring attack of their own, their frontcourt depth and overall ceiling may be limited by the lingering lower leg injury to star forward JT Toppin. In a fast-paced contest that projects to be a high-scoring affair, Alabama's marginally superior adjusted efficiency margin and overwhelming offensive firepower give them the narrow advantage needed to outpace Texas Tech and advance to the Sweet 16.

reasoningStep.title: Analyze Texas Tech vs Alabama Matchup
reasoningStep.summary: Alabama and Texas Tech are very evenly matched, with Alabama holding a slight edge in KenPom and Texas Tech in Torvik. Alabama's elite offense (#3) will test Texas Tech's balanced profile (#12 offense, #27 defense). The lingering injury to Texas Tech's JT Toppin gives Alabama a slight edge in a high-scoring game.
evidence: Alabama KenPom: 16, Torvik: 17. Texas Tech KenPom: 19, Torvik: 12.
Alabama AdjO: 3, AdjD: 67. Texas Tech AdjO: 12, AdjD: 27.
JT Toppin injury noted in prior round.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
